content.problemStatement:
Once upon a time, in a land far, far away... nevermind, we don't recall the rest of the story.

All we know is that Chef has a matrix of integers MMM with NNN rows and NNN columns; he wants to take a single element from each row and a single element from each column (NNN elements in total) and find their bitwise XOR. Find the set of all values he could obtain.
constraints: 0≤Mi,j≤1,0230 \le M_{i, j} \le 1,0230≤Mi,j​≤1,023 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
**Subtaks #3 (5 points):** 0≤Mi,j≤10 \le M_{i, j} \le 10≤Mi,j​≤1 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th of the following lines contains NNN space-separated integers Mi,1,Mi,2,…,Mi,NM_{i, 1}, M_{i, 2}, \dots, M_{i, N}Mi,1​,Mi,2​,…,Mi,N​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one or more space-separated integers in increasing order — the obtainable values.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We could choose M1,1=5M_{1, 1} = 5M1,1​=5 and M2,2=7M_{2, 2} = 7M2,2​=7; since we are only allowed to choose one element from each row and one from each column, we cannot choose anything else from rows 1, 2 and columns 1, 2, so we have to choose M3,3=0M_{3, 3} = 0M3,3​=0. Their XOR is 5⊕7⊕0=25 \oplus 7 \oplus 0 = 25⊕7⊕0=2.
Another combination could be M1,3=15M_{1, 3} = 15M1,3​=15, M2,1=19M_{2, 1} = 19M2,1​=19 and M3,2=0M_{3, 2} = 0M3,2​=0. Their XOR is 15⊕19⊕0=2815 \oplus 19 \oplus 0 = 2815⊕19⊕0=28.
